title: dracaena reflexa var. bakeri
intro: Dracaena reflexa var. bakeri, also known as the lucky bamboo or ribbon plant, is a variety of the Dracaena reflexa plant, native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. The plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds. Consuming the plant's leaves has been associated with several health benefits, such as reducing blood pressure and cholesterol levels, and improving digestion. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
